verses.text: The elders are to 'feed the flock of God,' not for filthy lucre nor as lords, but 'being ensamples to the flock.' Shepherd well. Leaders serve by example, not domineering.
The elders are to 'feed the flock of God,' not for filthy lucre nor as lords, but 'being ensamples to the flock.' Shepherd well. Leaders serve by example, not domineering.
The elders are to 'feed the flock of God,' not for filthy lucre nor as lords, but 'being ensamples to the flock.' Shepherd well. Leaders serve by example, not domineering.
'When the chief Shepherd shall appear, ye shall receive a crown of glory that fadeth not away.' Crown coming. Faithful shepherds await an unfading reward.
Younger, submit to elder; 'be clothed with humility,' for 'God resisteth the proud'; 'humble yourselves... that he may exalt you.' Humble dress. The lowly are lifted by God in due time.
Younger, submit to elder; 'be clothed with humility,' for 'God resisteth the proud'; 'humble yourselves... that he may exalt you.' Humble dress. The lowly are lifted by God in due time.
'Casting all your care upon him; for he careth for you.' Cast your care. God invites us to unload every anxiety on Him.
Be sober, vigilant; 'your adversary the devil, as a roaring lion, walketh about, seeking whom he may devour'; resist him, steadfast. Watch. Stand firm against the prowling enemy.
Be sober, vigilant; 'your adversary the devil, as a roaring lion, walketh about, seeking whom he may devour'; resist him, steadfast. Watch. Stand firm against the prowling enemy.
The God of all grace 'make you perfect, stablish, strengthen, settle you' after a little suffering. To Him be dominion. Grace will restore the tried.
The God of all grace 'make you perfect, stablish, strengthen, settle you' after a little suffering. To Him be dominion. Grace will restore the tried.
By Silvanus he writes briefly, exhorting them to stand in 'the true grace of God.' Greet one another with charity. Farewell. He ends in grace and peace.
